action.reasoning: Observation: Second model (m2.pt with seed=1, num_layers=5, emb_dim=400) reached best val AP=0.2782. First model (best.pt) reached 0.2810. Eval AP for first was 0.2753. Now I need to update predict.py to ensemble both checkpoints and submit. Analysis: Ensembling two diverse models typically gives 0.5-2% boost. Decision: Update predict.py to average predictions from both checkpoints, test locally, then submit eval.
action.command: ls -la checkpoints/ && python -c "import torch; c=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt',map_location='cpu'); print('best args:',c['args']); c=torch.load('checkpoints/m2.pt',map_location='cpu'); print('m2 args:',c['args'])"
